lint.command: Not configured in .factory/services.yaml (project has no ESLint setup; lint validation skipped)

blockingIssues.description: The original test-infrastructure commit paired packages/web vite ^6.0.0 with vitest ^4.1.0, which later caused TS2769 typecheck failures in the web Vite/Vitest config. The feature was not actually complete when first merged because required typecheck validation was skipped.

The follow-up Vite alignment left bun.lock out of sync with packages/web/package.json: the manifest declares vite ^7.0.0 while bun.lock still records the web workspace vite spec as ^7.3.1. The dependency update workflow was not fully carried through to the committed lockfile.

appliedUpdates.description: Added typecheck:api, typecheck:web, and typecheck commands so shared validation state now includes the TypeScript checks scrutiny actually relies on.

Updated .factory/library/environment.md to record that packages/web now uses Vite 7 with Vitest 4, and that the earlier Vite 6 + Vitest 4 pairing caused web config type errors on this macOS environment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether post-cleanup startup verification is mandatory for metadata-only cleanup tasks, or require workers to record a deviation when they skip that step.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The cleanup-dead-code-and-deps review found the worker skipped the skill's startup verification step but still reported followedProcedure: true.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Allow trivial env/refactor-only API changes to rely on existing automated coverage instead of requiring new tests-first work every time.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-env-consistency-anthropic review showed a two-line consistency change that reasonably reused existing tests, while the skill still mandates TDD for all API changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how workers should validate frontend/infrastructure tasks when test infrastructure is not yet available, and make explicit that typecheck must still run before claiming procedure compliance.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The root-error-boundary review needed to defer automated tests until infrastructure existed, and the test-infrastructure-setup review found the worker skipped typecheck while still reporting procedure compliance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mission guidance to reflect the current tooling reality: packages/web now uses Vite 7 with Vitest 4, and the API env-var convention should clarify when process.env is required versus when c.env/types may still exist.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews flagged stale Vite 6 guidance, and the env-consistency review noted AGENTS says 'use process.env consistently' while the codebase still has c.env.ENVIRONMENT and Hono binding types.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add the macOS Vitest workaround directly to the api-worker skill.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in mission AGENTS.md and effectively handled by the current packages/api test script (`bun run test` invokes Vitest through Node).

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a shared-state note that AGENTS still says the web package uses Vite 6.x.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the guidance/library updates already captured from the Vite mismatch reviews.

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat the root-error-boundary -> VAL-CROSS-006 feature mapping mismatch as a services/library update.
rejectedObservations.reason: belongs to orchestrator-owned mission metadata rather than factual runtime shared state.
